title: AGE2026 Day 1: rapamycin's new mechanism crowns geroscience conference
description: Matt Kaeberlein's Day 1 recap of AGE2026 frames rapamycin as the undisputed heavyweight of geroscience: novel 2026 data (Kell et al.) shows low-dose rapamycin reduces DNA double-strand-break burden in T cells and suppresses p21-mediated senescence — a fundamentally new mechanism beyond autophagy. Sessions also surfaced female reproductive aging, microplastics, NAD and circadian alignment as 2026 focal topics.

title: D+Q senolytic safety warning: dasatinib + quercetin causes brain demyelination in mice
description: University of Connecticut researchers report that the popular D+Q senolytic stack at doses used in longevity experiments triggered profound myelin loss in the corpus callosum of both young and old mice, mimicking multiple sclerosis or chemotherapy 'chemo brain'. The damage occurred fast and was worse in younger animals, urging caution on off-label and prophylactic D+Q regimens.

title: ARTAN Bio closes $200K decentralized seed via VitaDAO for nonsense-mutation suppressor platform
description: Florida-based ARTAN Bio raised a $200K seed through VitaDAO's decentralized science model to fund preclinical work on ARTAN-102, an engineered suppressor targeting nonsense mutations behind aging-related and genetic diseases. The raise — and its NFC Summit pitch in Lisbon — typifies the decentralized longevity finance model emerging alongside traditional VC.

title: Biotech ETFs stage major rally Thursday: ARKG +6.93%, XLV +3.07%, IBB +2.35%
description: Longevity-adjacent biotech ETFs rallied broadly Thursday, with ARK Genomic Revolution (ARKG) up 6.93%, XLV +3.07%, IDNA +3.70%, HTEC +3.92%, IBB +2.35%, XBI +2.77%, BBH +2.00% — the strongest single-session move of the week. NewLimit's $435M Series C, biotech M&A momentum and AGE2026 conference flow likely drove the bid.

title: Rapamycin enters reproductive medicine: Columbia VIBRANT and Cell Reports Medicine IVF data
description: Two clinical lines now anchor rapamycin in reproductive longevity: Columbia's VIBRANT trial (5 mg weekly x 12 weeks in 35-45 y.o. women) suggests ~20% reduction in monthly follicular loss, while Li et al.'s 100-patient Cell Reports Medicine IVF RCT showed clinical pregnancy rates of 50.0% (rapamycin) vs 28.2% (control). Together they translate decades of mTOR/ovarian-aging bench work into prescription-grade clinical practice.

title: Kaeberlein AGE2026 Day 1 recap: microplastics, rapamycin, NAD, quantum energy
description: Kaeberlein's Day 1 reaction to AGE2026: rapamycin retains heavyweight status across immune, ovarian, neurological and metabolic systems, with new 2026 data adding T-cell DNA protection as a novel mechanism. Microplastics, NAD chrono-pharmacology, and circadian alignment dominated the program.

title: ARTAN Bio raises $200K via VitaDAO for nonsense-mutation suppression platform
description: ARTAN Bio raised $200K in decentralized seed funding via VitaDAO to advance ARTAN-102 — an engineered tRNA suppressor targeting premature stop codons that drive a wide range of genetic and aging-related diseases.

title: D+Q senolytic combo causes severe brain demyelination in mice: UConn study
description: UConn researchers found dasatinib + quercetin at longevity doses triggered demyelination across the corpus callosum in young and old mice, disrupting neural communication — calling D+Q safety into question for prophylactic human use.

title: Rapamycin in endometriosis and fertility — ESSI clinical synthesis
description: A clinical synthesis from ESSI integrates Columbia's VIBRANT trial, the Li et al. Cell Reports Medicine IVF RCT and Ahmadi RIF trial into practical pulsed-rapamycin protocols for ovarian aging, recurrent IVF failure and endometriosis-associated infertility.

title: Sinclair-Tally Health evidence map: which interventions actually slow epigenetic aging in humans
description: The Sinclair / Adiv Johnson evidence map (Frontiers in Genetics) analyzed 41 human studies and identified exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diets, caloric restriction, omega-3s and multivitamins as interventions with positive next-generation epigenetic clock signal.

title: University of Nicosia awards David Sinclair honorary doctorate
description: The University of Nicosia held the ceremony for awarding Harvard professor David Sinclair an honorary doctoral degree, recognizing his contributions to aging biology and longevity science.

title: NewLimit $435M Series C — biggest pure longevity round, Phase 1 in 2027
description: NewLimit, co-founded by Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong, raised $435M to launch a Phase 1 trial of its first epigenetic reprogramming therapy for alcohol-related liver disease in 2027.

title: Oxford Aging Cell: rapamycin protects T cell DNA, rejuvenates immune system
description: Oxford's Kell lab reports that low-dose rapamycin reduces DNA double-strand breaks in T cells, doubles their survival after damage and lowers senescence markers in older adults — adding an immune-rejuvenation mechanism to mTOR's healthspan story.

title: University of Sydney 4-week diet RCT reduces biological age in 65-75 y.o.
description: A 104-person Aging Cell RCT shows three of four dietary patterns reduced biological-age scores after just 4 weeks in adults aged 65-75; the higher-carb omnivore arm produced the largest improvement.

title: GLP-1 semaglutide DunedinPACE -9% — SciTechDaily and global press
description: SciTechDaily and Yahoo Health continue to amplify UC San Diego's randomized placebo-controlled trial showing semaglutide slowed DunedinPACE biological aging by 9% and reduced PCGrimAge mortality markers across multiple epigenetic clocks.

title: Two national canine antiaging trials: rapamycin and LOY-002
description: In dogs, two national clinical trials of antiaging drugs are underway in parallel: rapamycin and Loyal's LOY-002 — a more mysterious metabolic geroprotector. Healthspan readouts would lend support to use in other species, including humans.

title: Cyclarity UDP-003 Phase 1: first-in-human oxidized cholesterol excretion data
description: Cyclarity Therapeutics presented Phase 1 data at AHA Vascular Discovery showing UDP-003 — a cyclodextrin targeting 7-ketocholesterol — produced measurable oxidized cholesterol excretion in humans, a first proof of concept for clearing the soft-plaque lipid driver.

title: Daily Rounds — Longevity & Healthcare Brief, May 31, 2026
description: Healthcare Discovery AI's Daily Rounds longevity brief synthesizes the week's most relevant geroscience, biotech and protocol news for clinicians and longevity-focused operators.

title: CNBC: 2026 biotech deals reach $106B across 201 transactions YTD
description: CNBC reports biotech M&A reached $106B across 201 deals YTD 2026 — a resurgent capital cycle that likely underwrote Thursday's biotech ETF rally.

title: PEARL trial: first long-term human rapamycin RCT shows muscle and bone benefits
description: The PEARL rapamycin RCT reports no serious adverse events across 48 weeks of weekly rapamycin in older adults; 10 mg/week preserved muscle in women and bone mineral content in men.

title: BioEdge Longevity Daily News scan
description: BioEdge Longevity's daily news scan tracks ongoing geroscience pipeline updates, social media discussion and clinical-trial reports — a useful at-a-glance feed of the field.

title: Global Longevity Federation: rapamycin lecture and HeSReC launch
description: Global Longevity Federation news digest features the April 'Rapamycin Story' lecture, Tohoku University's HeSReC launch and McGill's longevity therapy / fall-prevention findings.

title: Bryan Johnson 2026 supplement stack walkthrough — Naturecan
description: Naturecan's walkthrough of Bryan Johnson's 2026 supplement stack details dosages (NMN, NR, Ca-AKG, fisetin, resveratrol, K2-MK4, lithium, lion's mane, garlic, etc.) plus bi-weekly rapamycin rotation between 6 mg and 13 mg.

title: Bryan Johnson Blueprint peptide stack 2026 — Fast Life Hacks
description: Updated peptide protocol covers Blueprint Peptide Serum (eight biomimetic peptides applied to the scalp), 20-30 g/day collagen peptides with vitamin C, and an Epithalon/Thymulin 10 mg IM x 5 days every six months protocol.

title: Peter Attia: resistance training — lowering the barrier to entry
description: Peter Attia synthesizes a meta-analysis showing LM2 (low-load, multiple sets, 2x/week) protocols capture most of strength, hypertrophy and mobility benefits in untrained adults — a minimum-effective protocol for sarcopenia prevention.

title: Peter Attia Drive #394: sleep pharmacology and Alzheimer's prevention
description: Attia's new podcast episode dives into sleep pharmacology, prescription sleep medications and their effects on sleep architecture, and the promise of dual orexin receptor antagonists (DORAs) for Alzheimer's prevention.

title: Fight Aging June 1 newsletter: ProAL50, multi-omics clocks, public health
description: Fight Aging's June 1 digest leads with the ProAL50 proteomic allostatic-load index, multi-omics clock convergence, nanomaterials in osteoarthritis cartilage and the genetics of exceptional longevity.

title: Engineering Post primer: senolytics target zombie cells
description: Engineering Post primer summarizes the senolytic class, including senescent-cell clearance and emerging evidence on tissue health, muscle function and biological-age markers — with reminders that long-term safety needs confirmation.

title: Mass General Brigham thymus paper continues to ripple in aging press
description: Follow-on coverage continues on MGB's Nature paper showing AI-scored thymic fat ratio on chest CT independently predicts mortality, cardiovascular death and cancer outcomes — a new candidate aging biomarker.

title: Bar-Ilan Sirt6 paper: rejuvenation of mouse livers continues to attract press
description: Bar-Ilan University's paper showing reversal of liver aging in mice via SIRT6 elevation continues to attract international coverage, with SirTLab developing small-molecule activators.
